hash_format_notice: Manifest hash placeholders in this corpus are synthetic, derived deterministically. Top-level hash fields (tally_algorithm_hash, eligibility_snapshot_hash, action_set_hash, tally_input_hash, tally_output_hash, result_hash) use the seed pattern SHA-256 of 'actproof.testvector.corporate.board_resolution_v1.<tv_id>.<field>'. Attachment hashes use the seed pattern SHA-256 of 'actproof.testvector.corporate.board_resolution_v1.<tv_id>.attachment.<label>'. Compact note r and e values use the same seed pattern with field names compact_note.r and compact_note.e, raw bytes base64url-encoded without padding. The vector ids in the seed strings are the short forms tv01, tv02, tv03. Hashes have the correct cryptographic shape (32-byte SHA-256, hex or base64url-encoded) but are not computed from canonical manifest inputs. Corpus v1.1 will replace these with reproducible hashes computed from documented canonical inputs in a companion test-vectors/inputs/ directory. The catalogue-conformance verification path exercised by these vectors is independent of the cryptographic verification path and does not require resolved inputs.
verifier_result_code_notice: Test vectors 2 and 3 use result codes FAIL_MANIFEST_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED and FAIL_MANIFEST_QUORUM_BELOW_THRESHOLD that are proposed for inclusion in v1.4 of the master spec amendment, not present in v1.3. Master spec amendment v1.3 section 9.6 defines PASS_WITH_SEMANTICS as requiring the manifest to conform to the catalogue entry's required fields and method constraints, but does not define explicit failure codes for the negative cases. Implementations against v1.3 may report these failures as PASS_WITH_SEMANTICS with a structured conformance result field, or as an unstructured fail. Implementations against v1.4 are expected to use the explicit codes.

test_vectors.description: A corporate board resolution by the board of directors of a fictitious international private holding company, authorizing a working capital credit facility, using simple majority voting with the catalogue minimum quorum, with all required manifest fields present and the three substantive attachments included. Expected to produce PASS_WITH_SEMANTICS.
test_vectors.scenario_narrative: On 16 June 2026 at 09:00 UTC, the board of directors of Meridian Capital Partners, a fictitious international private holding company, adopted a resolution authorizing management to enter into a USD 50 million revolving credit facility for working capital purposes. The board has 7 directors. The vote used simple_majority_v1 with quorum at 5001 basis points (more than 50% of directors present) and threshold at 5001 basis points (more than 50% of votes cast in favour). All three required attachments are present: the resolution document itself, the supporting documentation including the credit facility term sheet and proposed loan agreement, and the financial impact analysis prepared by the CFO's office.
test_vectors.rationale: All thirteen required_manifest_fields are present in the manifest. The method_id simple_majority_v1 is in the allowed_method_ids list. The quorum_basis_points value of 5001 meets the catalogue minimum of 5001 (the boundary case). The decision_type is board_resolution, the tenant_id and system_created_at are present, and three attachment_hashes entries are present including the load-bearing resolution_document. The action_set_hash represents the resolution text being approved (the credit facility authorization). The compact note carries the act-type identifier in disclosed mode with version, type, merkle root, envelope hash, manifest count, and s field all conforming to section 9.2 of the amendment. Under v1.3 section 9.6 semantics, the verifier returns PASS_WITH_SEMANTICS.

test_vectors.description: A claimed corporate board resolution using a method (plurality_v1) that is not in the catalogue entry's allowed_method_ids list. The cryptographic proof path is intact and the s field is well-formed, but the manifest fails catalogue conformance. Expected to produce FAIL_MANIFEST_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED (proposed v1.4 code).
test_vectors.scenario_narrative: The same board of directors attempts to record a resolution using plurality_v1 (first-past-the-post without majority requirement) rather than one of the four approval-family methods the catalogue entry permits. Plurality without majority does not satisfy the substantive corporate governance requirement that a board resolution represents the will of more than half the directors present and voting. Under most corporate-law frameworks and standard articles of incorporation, a board resolution requires majority support; plurality could allow a minority position to prevail when votes are split across multiple options.
test_vectors.rationale: All required fields are present, the s field is well-formed, and the catalogue entry resolves. The single point of failure is that the manifest's method_parameters.method_id of plurality_v1 is not in the catalogue entry's allowed_method_ids list. The checks_performed list does not include quorum_basis_points_at_or_above_minimum because the method check fails first and downstream checks are short-circuited in this corpus's verifier model. This negative case demonstrates that the catalogue entry encodes the substantive corporate-governance requirement (collective board decision by an approval-family method that produces a binary outcome) as a mechanically-checkable constraint, even for a non-statutory act type that derives from generic articles of incorporation rather than a specific regulatory instrument.

test_vectors.description: A claimed corporate board resolution using an allowed method (simple_majority_v1) but with a quorum threshold of 4000 basis points, below the catalogue entry's minimum_quorum_basis_points of 5001. Expected to produce FAIL_MANIFEST_QUORUM_BELOW_THRESHOLD (proposed v1.4 code).
test_vectors.scenario_narrative: The same board of directors attempts to record a resolution where the decision-rules quorum is 40% of the board. The substantive corporate governance requirement is that a board resolution represents the will of more than half the directors. A 40% quorum rule means a resolution could be adopted with only three of seven directors present and voting in favour, which does not satisfy the substantive requirement of majority board decisional power. The catalogue entry encodes this floor as minimum_quorum_basis_points = 5001 (more than 50% of board members).
test_vectors.rationale: All required fields are present, the s field is well-formed, the catalogue entry resolves, and the method id is in the allowed list. The single point of failure is that the manifest's method_parameters.quorum_basis_points of 4000 falls below the catalogue entry's minimum_quorum_basis_points of 5001. This negative case demonstrates that the catalogue entry encodes the substantive corporate-governance requirement (board majority decisional power requires majority presence) as a numeric constraint applicable across jurisdictions, even though the act type itself is not bound to a specific regulatory instrument. The expected_combined_verifier_result is proposed for v1.4 of the master spec amendment.

gaps_surfaced_for_v1_4_amendment.description: The schema's regulatory_citation block was designed for statutory instruments with concrete article references. Generic corporate governance acts under articles of incorporation, customary law, contractual frameworks, or international standards do not fit cleanly. This entry uses 'n/a' for article and 'Universal' for jurisdiction as workarounds rather than vocabulary-supported values. A v1.4 schema iteration should add an optional regulatory_citation.basis_type enum with values 'statutory', 'contractual', 'customary', 'international_standard', to mark non-statutory entries explicitly. The article field could become optional when basis_type is not 'statutory'.

The schema uses jurisdiction strings like 'EU' (NIS2 and AI Act entries) and now 'Universal' (this entry), but there is no formal vocabulary defining valid jurisdiction values. ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes work for national jurisdictions, 'EU' is a convention for European Union instruments, but 'Universal' or 'Global' for cross-jurisdictional entries is not formally defined. A v1.4 schema iteration should define the jurisdiction vocabulary explicitly, including special values for supranational ('EU', 'UN') and universal ('Universal' or '*') cases, with ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes for national jurisdictions and ISO 3166-2 codes for subnational jurisdictions where relevant.

The in_force_from field is defined as 'the date on which obligated entities first become subject to the requirement'. For statutory acts this is clear and authoritative. For board resolutions under articles of incorporation, the obligation arises whenever a company exists with valid articles, which varies per company. Using the catalogue publication date (2026-05-13) is a workaround but conflates the codification-availability date with the regulatory-effective date. A v1.4 schema iteration should add an optional in_force_basis field with values like 'statutory_commencement', 'codification_publication', 'entity_specific_articles', to make the semantics of the date field explicit per entry.
